title: AMQP 0-9-1 Topology and Routing Models · Program Defense
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for amqp 0-9-1 topology and routing models and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an executable Go topology and destination model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace producers, connections, channels, exchanges, bindings, queues, consumers, deliveries, and acknowledgements without collapsing their roles; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Use virtual hosts as separate topology and permission namespaces while recognizing they are not complete process or resource isolation; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Derive destination sets from exchange type, routing key, binding key, headers, and exchange-to-exchange bindings; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Amqp Entity Model
steps.instruction:
Order the Amqp Entity Model RabbitMQ and Go messaging evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Amqp Entity Model.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Amqp Entity Model assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For RabbitMQ and Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Amqp Entity Model, keep Amqp Entity Model connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.text: A quiz Go messaging team handles case 4269 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive zero, one, or many queue destinations from AMQP exchange and binding values; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace producers, connections, channels, exchanges, bindings, queues, consumers, deliveries, and acknowledgements without collapsing their roles; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

content.text: Order work so Amqp Entity Model has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Amqp Error Scope
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Amqp Error Scope: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 6602 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive zero, one, or many queue destinations from AMQP exchange and binding values; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify connection-level, channel-level, topology precondition, return, confirm, and consumer cancellation outcomes by recovery scope; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Amqp Error Scope.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Amqp Error Scope, keep Amqp Error Scope connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Classify connection-level, channel-level, topology precondition, return, confirm, and consumer cancellation outcomes by recovery scope; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Amqp Error Scope, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Amqp Error Scope precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Amqp Error Scope, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A quiz Go messaging team handles case 6602 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive zero, one, or many queue destinations from AMQP exchange and binding values; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify connection-level, channel-level, topology precondition, return, confirm, and consumer cancellation outcomes by recovery scope; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Amqp Error Scope, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Every AMQP error requires terminating the process and rebuilding the entire connection. In Go, an amqp091 Publishing never names a destination queue directly.

checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
